page: Microsoft Azure E32ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epsv6 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is powered by a dedicated Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The configuration features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, resulting in a single thread per core. Memory capacity is 256.0 GB, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 128 MB L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The server achieved top-tier results in large-block memory bandwidth tests, with read-only speeds reaching 436.06 GB/sec and write speeds at 184.19 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is strong, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 66,283.59 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 19,664.0. Database operations are highly efficient, with Passmark database operations scoring in the top tier and Redis SET operations reaching 61.18 million operations per second. However, single-core performance remains average, and large-scale language model inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model registering a poor score of 4.66 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32ps_v6 offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. Given the top-tier memory bandwidth and database benchmark results, this server is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks. It is less suitable for workloads requiring advanced vector extensions or heavy large-model AI inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine powered by a dedicated 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 32 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-compute ratio. The server does not include local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds of 436.06 GB/sec and write speeds of 184.19 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-core performance. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank highly. However, single-core execution is average, and large-model LLM inference is slow. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database management, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, suited for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32ps_v6 is a 32-vCPU ARM virtual machine with 256 GB RAM, offering top-tier memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring high memory density, top-tier memory bandwidth, and strong multi-core efficiency for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor with 32 dedicated cores
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-compute ratio
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth with 436.06 GB/sec large-block read speeds
Achieves strong multi-core performance and high-throughput database operations
Lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage
Suited for in-memory databases, caching systems, and multi-threaded web serving
